steps.title: Identify the subject and separate modifiers
steps.content: The subject is “the triangle.” The phrase “representing the mountain itself” is a participial phrase that modifies the subject but is not part of it. The verb must agree with the main subject, not the participial phrase.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: ”The triangle” is singular, so the verb must be singular. “Is” is the singular present-tense form that correctly agrees. The other choices—”are,” “have been,” and “were”—are plural verb forms that violate subject-verb agreement. That confirms D is correct.
